title: Rocket Engine Design
shortSummary: Designing engines that work — turbopumps, injectors, cooling systems, and the engineering trade-offs behind Merlin, Raptor, and RS-25.
whyItMatters: The rocket engine is the most complex and performance-critical component of any launch vehicle. SpaceX's Raptor engine represents decades of engineering evolution. Understanding turbopump cycles, regenerative cooling, and injector design separates theoretical knowledge from engineering capability.
coreConcepts: Gas generator vs staged combustion vs full-flow staged combustion
